title: Pedestrian killed in Roxbury hit-and-run
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A pedestrian was killed in a hit-and-run crash near Southampton and Bradston Streets in Roxbury on Friday morning.
The victim was pronounced dead at a local hospital. The involved vehicle has been found, authorities said.
The driver fled the scene and no arrests have been made as the investigation by Boston police continues.

title: Here’s what meteorologists are saying about chances for snow through Monday
author: Jack Pickell
bullets: Boston could see a coating to 2 inches of wet snow, especially near I-90, through Monday.
Rain is expected to switch to wet snow and sleet in parts of northern Massachusetts through southeastern regions.
Snow showers on grassy surfaces and hills expected Monday morning and evening, according to local forecasts.

title: Mass. police officer arrested for OUI, department says
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Norwood police officer Shawn Willman was arrested and charged with OUI while off duty, according to department officials.
Willman has been placed on administrative leave pending the outcome of the criminal case and an internal investigation.
The Norwood Police Department emphasized their commitment to high standards of conduct and accountability for their officers.

title: Police: Mass. man allegedly stabbed friend during fight over woman at Miami Beach bachelor party
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Olajuwon Shawn Dickerson, 32, from Cape Cod, was charged with aggravated battery after allegedly stabbing his friend in Miami Beach.
Incident occurred at a bachelor party, where an argument escalated over women's attention leading to a stabbing.
Police connected a folding knife, allegedly belonging to Dickerson, to the injuries sustained by both men.

title: 4 takeaways from the Revolution’s 3-1 loss to St. Louis City SC
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution suffered a 3-1 defeat against St. Louis City SC, marking their third loss in four 2026 season games.
Chris Durkin scored the go-ahead goal from a corner kick for St. Louis at the end of the first half.
New England forward Leo Campana exited the game in the 80th minute due to a new, unspecified injury.

title: Don’t be fooled by this fake court notice scam in Mass., NH, authorities warn
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Massachusetts and New Hampshire residents warned about fake court notices demanding fines for traffic violations.
Scam documents use incorrect state names and mismatched contact details, lacking verified case information.
Officials urge recipients to verify unexpected communications and avoid providing personal information or scanning QR codes.

title: The trial of Kelsey Fitzsimmons begins this week. Here’s what to know.
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons, a North Andover police officer, was shot by a colleague during an armed confrontation in June 2025.
Fitzsimmons, accused of assault with a dangerous weapon, pleaded not guilty; her trial starts March 23, 2026.
The bench trial, without a jury, will be overseen by Judge Jeffrey Karp in Essex Superior Court.

title: She’s running Boston for patients ‘rebuilding their lives step by step’
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Lina Sinisterra Mazariegos will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for Spaulding Rehabilitation Hospital, inspired by patients' recovery journeys.
Mazariegos views marathons as a metaphor for life, emphasizing patience, humility, and persevering through challenges.
Her run aims to honor those rebuilding independence and to support professionals aiding in recovery at Spaulding Rehabilitation.

title: Review & Setlist: ‘We are foreign imports, so we have paid tariffs.’
author: Marc Hirsh
bullets: The Hives performed at Boston's House of Blues with support from Australian band The Chats on March 20, 2026.
The Hives showcased both new and classic songs, maintaining energy and engagement throughout their set.
Frontman Howlin' Pelle Almqvist led the performance with dynamic showmanship, engaging the audience with interactive segments.

title: Cancer couldn’t stop her. Now she’s running the Boston Marathon again.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Stephanie McHugh, 42, from Braintree, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for the New England Patriots Foundation.
After a Stage 3 breast cancer diagnosis in 2023, McHugh overcame chemotherapy and surgeries to return to running.
McHugh runs to honor friends, including her late mentor Susan Hurley, and to show cancer doesn't get the final word.
